skill.name: gpt-image-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with OpenAI GPT Image 2 (ChatGPT Images 2.0) on RunComfy. Documents GPT Image 2's strengths (embedded text, logos, multilingual typography, instruction precision), its 3 fixed sizes, edit-with-preservation language, and when to route to a sibling (Flux 2 / Nano Banana Pro / Seedream) instead. Calls `runcomfy run openai/gpt-image-2/text-to-image` or `/edit` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "gpt image 2", "gpt-image-2", "ChatGPT Images 2", "image 2", or any explicit ask to generate or edit with this model.
skill.summary: Documentation skill for OpenAI GPT Image 2 on RunComfy — generates and edits images with precise text rendering and directive following.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate documentation skill that describes how to use the GPT Image 2 model via the RunComfy CLI. All 77 static findings are false positives: the scanner mistook bash code blocks in markdown documentation for actual command execution, flagged first-party API endpoints as hardcoded URLs, confused sysexits exit codes with cryptographic algorithms, and misread API path placeholders as path traversal. The skill contains a thorough security and privacy section documenting token storage, input boundaries, and outbound endpoints.

low_findings.title: Shell command instructions in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash code blocks showing how to invoke the `runcomfy` CLI (e.g. lines 24, 74-89, 93-100, 106-109). These are documentation examples, not executable code — the skill itself does not execute commands. Users must manually run the commands. No shell injection risk from prompt content as the CLI transmits JSON bodies directly over HTTPS without shell expansion.

low_findings.title: Outbound network calls to RunComfy API
low_findings.description: The documented workflow makes HTTPS requests to model-api.runcomfy.net and downloads results from *.runcomfy.net / *.runcomfy.com. All endpoints are first-party to the RunComfy service the skill is designed to use. No telemetry, no callbacks, no data exfiltration. The skill explicitly documents the outbound endpoint scope in its Security & Privacy section.

low_findings.title: Token stored in local config file
low_findings.description: The RunComfy CLI stores API tokens at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with 0600 permissions. This is a standard config location, not a hidden or suspicious path. The skill documents the option to use RUNCOMFY_TOKEN env var to bypass the file in CI/container environments.

content.user_title: Generate precise images with GPT Image 2 on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Creating images with embedded text, logos, and multilingual typography is unreliable with most models. This skill routes requests to GPT Image 2 via RunComfy CLI for directive-precise image generation and editing.
actual_capabilities: Generates images from text prompts via the openai/gpt-image-2/text-to-image endpoint
Edits existing images using natural-language instructions via the openai/gpt-image-2/edit endpoint
Renders embedded text, logos, and multilingual typography with high accuracy
Supports up to 10 reference images for multi-ref composition edits
Provides 3 fixed output sizes (1024x1024, 1024x1536, 1536x1024) with auto-ratio preservation on edits
Documents when to route to sibling models (Flux 2, Nano Banana Pro, Seedream) for different use cases
limitations: Only supports 3 fixed sizes on text-to-image; extreme aspect ratios are auto-resized
Long blocks of embedded text degrade output quality
Multi-image edit support is guidance-based, not ControlNet-style stacking
Photorealistic portraits are not its strongest capability — Nano Banana Pro excels there

use_cases.title: E-commerce product photography
use_cases.description: Generate product hero images with accurate brand text on labels and consistent lighting across SKUs.

use_cases.title: Multilingual signage and ad creation
use_cases.description: Create storefront signage, posters, and ad creatives with embedded text in multiple scripts including Japanese, Cyrillic, and Arabic.

use_cases.title: Iterative image editing with composition stability
use_cases.description: Refine images by changing one attribute at a time while keeping composition stable, using edit-with-preservation language.

prompt_templates.title: Basic text-to-image generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image with GPT Image 2: a minimal hero product still life with a matte ceramic water bottle on warm linen, soft window light, the word "AQUA+" in clean sans-serif on the label, subtle rim highlights, e-commerce ready.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user generating a product photo

prompt_templates.title: Multilingual signage generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 to create a small Tokyo cafe storefront at dusk with warm interior glow. The sign reads "コーヒー" in bold Japanese kana on a wooden plaque. Apply shallow depth of field and rule of thirds composition.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating localized signage with non-Latin scripts

prompt_templates.title: Background swap with element preservation
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit the provided image with GPT Image 2: turn the background into a bright minimal white-to-soft-gray studio sweep with gentle floor shadow. Add a large headline that reads "OPEN STUDIO" in bold clean sans-serif, centered. Keep the main person, pose, and face identity unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: Editing a photo while preserving the subject

prompt_templates.title: Multi-reference composition edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 edit with 2 reference images: compose the subject from image 1 into the room from image 2. Match the lighting of image 2. Keep the subject pose and identity from image 1 unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced multi-image composition for product placement

output_examples.input: Generate a product hero image: matte ceramic water bottle, warm linen, word "AQUA+" on label, e-commerce style.
output_examples.output: A studio-quality product photo is generated and saved to the specified output directory. The image shows a matte ceramic water bottle on warm linen with soft window light, neutral background, and the text "AQUA+" rendered clearly in clean sans-serif on the label.

output_examples.input: Edit: change background to white studio sweep, add "OPEN STUDIO" headline, keep person and pose unchanged.
output_examples.output: An edited image is returned with the background replaced by a white-to-soft-gray studio sweep. The headline "OPEN STUDIO" is rendered in bold sans-serif, centered, with high contrast. The original person, pose, and face identity are preserved.

best_practices: Quote embedded text exactly in single or double quotes to get accurate text rendering from GPT Image 2.
Iterate one attribute at a time (lighting, background, pose, or text) while keeping the rest of the prompt verbatim to maintain composition stability.
State preservation goals explicitly in edit prompts using words like "keep" and "unchanged" to tell the model what not to modify.
anti_patterns: Do not pile up conflicting style instructions like "ukiyo-e + watercolor + 8K + cinematic + minimalist" — they cancel out. Pick one or two style anchors.
Do not give contradictory instructions such as "no text" combined with "the word 'AQUA+' on the label" — the model picks one and you lose control.
Do not use this model for heavy stylization or painterly looks — route to Flux 2 instead for those aesthetics.

faq.question: What is GPT Image 2?
faq.answer: GPT Image 2 (also called ChatGPT Images 2.0) is an OpenAI image generation model with strong directive precision. It excels at embedded text rendering, logos, multilingual typography, and following multi-element prompts accurately.

faq.question: Do I need an OpenAI API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses the RunComfy Model API, which hosts GPT Image 2. You need a RunComfy account and CLI installed, but no OpenAI key is required.

faq.question: How do I install the prerequisites?
faq.answer: Install the RunComfy CLI globally with npm i -g @runcomfy/cli, then run runcomfy login to authenticate via browser device-code flow. In CI environments, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: What image sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Three fixed sizes: 1024x1024 (1:1 square), 1024x1536 (2:3 portrait), and 1536x1024 (3:2 landscape). The edit endpoint also supports size=auto to preserve the input aspect ratio.

faq.question: Can I use this for multilingual text in images?
faq.answer: Yes. GPT Image 2 handles multilingual typography well. Name the script explicitly in your prompt, for example: Japanese kana, Cyrillic, or Arabic right-to-left. Keep embedded text short for best results.

faq.question: When should I use a different model instead?
faq.answer: Use Flux 2 for heavy stylization and painterly looks. Use Nano Banana Pro for hyperrealistic portraits. Use Seedream 5 for cinematic or aesthetic-first hero shots. Use GPT Image 2 when what is on the canvas matters more than how stylized it looks.
